data_description.project_name: Discovery-Neuromodulator circuit dynamics during foraging - Subproject 3 Fiber Photometry Recordings of NM Release During Behavior

channel.description: Isosbestic control emission (510nm) with UV excitation (415nm)
Isosbestic control emission (510nm) with UV excitation (415nm)
Isosbestic control emission (510nm) with UV excitation (415nm)
Isosbestic control emission (510nm) with UV excitation (415nm)

data_streams.notes: Behavioral tracking with IR LED This record has been updated to include intended measurements. Fiber connections are repeated for fibers with multiple channels. Disconnected fibers are marked with fiber_name 'disconnected'.

stimulus_parameters.notes: assigned reward probability is 10%, duration is 1.0 s
assigned reward probability is 50%, duration is 1.0 s
assigned reward probability is 90%, duration is 1.0 s
White noise, aversive probability is 90%, duration is 1.0 s

starting_position.notes: Left spout in extended position, manually positioned about 1.5 mm from the lower jaw
Right spout in retracted position, retraction travel is 30 mm placing it about 31.5 mm from the lower jaw.

reward_delivery.notes: Water delivered from left lick spout, right spout retracted

session.notes: The following information has no corresponding field in the session metadata: Punishment delivery: airpuff with 25 psi for 1 s (DO2).

stage_type.name: NewScaleMotor for LickSpouts
stage_type.model: XYZ Stage with M30LS-3.4-15 linear stages

lens.name: Behavior Video Lens Bottom of face

filters.name: beamsplitter
filters.notes: 493/574 nm BrightLine dual-edge standard epi-fluorescence dichroic beamsplitter

daqs.name: harp behavior board
daqs.notes: Left lick spout and Right lick spout, as well as reward delivery solenoids are connected via ethernet cables

calibrations.description: Water calibration for Lick spout Left. The input is the valve open time in seconds and the output is the volume of water delivered in microliters.
Water calibration for Lick spout Right. The input is the valve open time in seconds and the output is the volume of water delivered in microliters.
